prompt_queue.prompt: Maintenance pass for technical, developer-facing living documentation. Review the repository source code, tests, existing docs, recent issues, and recent pull requests for documentation gaps, drift, fragmentation, unclear developer guidance, and documentation information architecture problems. Treat existing Future coverage, deferred coverage, out-of-scope, and follow-up sections as a documentation backlog. When source evidence is available and scope fits the run budget, replace deferred bullets with written documentation pages or sections, then remove the satisfied deferred items. Keep only genuinely deferred items with clear reasons. When a workspace is provided, inspect and edit documentation through workspace tools, then run workspace_git_status and workspace_git_diff before finishing. Preserve or improve the repository's existing docs taxonomy, index convention, and topic grouping. For source-code GitHub repositories, docs/ is the default technical documentation surface unless the consumer prompt declares a different audience namespace. Prefer focused updates that keep existing docs aligned with current code behavior across public APIs, internal processes, module connections, extension points, lifecycle, data contracts, auth/permissions, errors and failure modes, examples, operational workflows, compatibility boundaries, and software philosophy. Use no_changes when existing docs are accurate, sufficiently broad for the changed or inspected areas, future/deferred coverage has no bounded item ready to document, and the docs are easy to navigate.
